description_long: This lamppost features a tall, slender column with a twisted, spiral shaft that adds a decorative touch to its otherwise classical form. The base is square and robust, providing stability, while the top transitions into a capital adorned with ornate, leafy carvings reminiscent of Corinthian architectural styles. The shaft appears to be made of a stone-like material, giving it a solid and durable appearance suitable for outdoor urban environments. The capital is the most visually intricate part, with detailed relief work that stands out against the smoother, spiraled column. There is no visible lamp head or light fixture attached to the top, suggesting it may be a decorative or historical lamppost base. The overall color is a uniform light beige, enhancing its stone-like aesthetic and blending well with traditional urban or park settings.
description: A tall, spiral-column lamppost with a square base and ornate, leafy capital, finished in light beige stone-like material. No visible lamp head or attachments.
description_view_0: Front view shows the full height, spiral shaft, and detailed leafy capital clearly.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated, the spiral pattern and ornate capital remain prominent, with subtle shadowing on the shaft.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to the front, highlighting the uniform spiral and decorative top.
description_view_3: Back view mirrors the front, with the spiral shaft and ornate capital visible, no additional features.
attribute_lamp_head_design: No lamp head present; only a decorative capital with leafy carvings.
